title: Hong Kong eMPF Migration Planning and Asia OKR Alignment
situation: Hong Kong's MPF market was undergoing a regulator-led migration to the eMPF Platform, a centralized administration system that fundamentally changes the operating model for MPF trustees. Manulife's Asia retirement business needed business planning, forecasting, and operating measures that reflected the migration's commercial impact. At the same time, the Asia team's KPIs and OKRs were not consistently aligned with the rest of Global Retirement, making it difficult to compare performance or coordinate strategy across regions.
obstacle: The eMPF migration introduced significant uncertainty into revenue, cost, and operating dynamics for the HK business. Forecasting under that uncertainty required close partnership with the Asia team. Separately, aligning Asia OKRs with Global Retirement required building shared definitions without overriding regional context that the Asia team rightly owned.
actions: Partnered with Hong Kong leadership on business planning and forecasting for the eMPF migration
Worked closely with the Asia team to develop OKRs and KPIs consistent with the rest of Global Retirement
Built shared definitions and measurement standards while respecting regional context and ownership
Integrated Asia performance into global reporting in a way that preserved comparability
results: Business planning and forecasting framework established for the eMPF transition
Asia OKRs and KPIs aligned with Global Retirement, improving cross-regional comparability and strategic coordination
Strengthened the analytical relationship between the Asia team and the global insights function
fullStory: Two parallel tracks of Asia work. First, the eMPF migration in Hong Kong is a generational change for the market and required rebuilding the business planning and forecasting view of the HK retirement business. I partnered with HK leadership on both. Second, Asia OKRs and KPIs were not consistently aligned with the rest of Global Retirement, which made cross-regional strategy harder than it needed to be. I worked with the Asia team to build a consistent measurement framework without flattening regional context. The combination established a stronger analytical foundation for the Asia retirement business at a moment when it needed one.
